mnemonics.title: Sequential Search Complexity
mnemonics.content: Worst: O(n) - search all; Best: O(1) - found first; Average: O(n/2) - middle. Remember: 'Worst Needs N, Best Is 1, Average is Half Way Through'.

mnemonics.title: Brute Force String Matching
mnemonics.content: Worst: O(nm) - compare all everywhere; Best: O(n) - mismatch first char. Remember: 'Brute Force is Never Perfect - Worst Needs n×m, Best Needs n Only'.

acronyms.usage: Algorithm design technique that tries all possibilities
Linear search through list element by element
Pattern matching algorithm with O(n+m) complexity using preprocessing

visualTips.visualization: Think of a row of lockers (array elements). You check locker 1, then 2, then 3... until you find what you want or reach the end. Each check is one comparison.
Imagine a sliding window (pattern) moving across the text. At each position, you compare character by character, like checking if two passwords match character by character at each offset.

analogies.analogy: Like searching for a specific book on an unsorted shelf - you must look at each book one by one from left to right.
Like trying a key on every lock, and for each lock, trying each tooth position - exhaustive trial of all possibilities.
